summary: Iteration 4 (mobile UI/UX polish) verified end-to-end. Backend regression: 21/21 pytest PASS + manual smoke on /api/demo, /api/matches/{id} (200), /api/matches/{id}/mvp/results (200), /api/history/5551000002 (200), manifest.json (200), favicon.svg (200). Frontend mobile (390x844): hamburger + Create pill visible, desktop nav-links hidden; opening hamburger reveals data-testid='nav-menu' with 'nav-my-matches-mobile' and 'nav-history-mobile' — both navigate correctly and auto-close the menu. Desktop (1200x800): hamburger and nav-create-mobile hidden; nav-my-matches / nav-history / nav-create all visible and route correctly. Zero horizontal overflow at 390px on /, /create, /my-matches, /history. Touch targets all >=44px except a single Home CTA (demo-open-btn = 42px) — minor.

issues: 'OPEN MATCH PAGE' button (data-testid='demo-open-btn') measured 42px tall on mobile viewport — 2px below the stated 44px min-touch-target rule. All other .tap elements comply (nav-home 48px, nav-create-mobile 44px, nav-menu-toggle 44px, hero-create-btn 52px, hero-history-btn 54px).

action_items: Minor: bump demo-open-btn on Home to h-11 (44px) or add min-h-[44px] so it meets the mobile touch-target rule the polish pass introduced.
critical_code_review_comments: Nav implementation is clean: sticky header, useLocation-driven auto-close on route change, aria-expanded on hamburger, distinct data-testids for desktop and mobile (nav-my-matches vs nav-my-matches-mobile) — good for tests.
InstallButton correctly returns null when beforeinstallprompt hasn't fired; both desktop and mobile variants use .tap and correct testids.
Consider adding role='navigation' to the mobile dropdown (data-testid='nav-menu') and trapping focus while open — not blocking, just an a11y polish.
seed_data_creation: None — reused demo match for smoke.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile nav uses data-testids nav-create-mobile, nav-menu-toggle, nav-menu, nav-my-matches-mobile, nav-history-mobile; desktop uses nav-create, nav-my-matches, nav-history. Overflow check at 390px is a good regression guard for future layout tweaks. If a functional flow needs deeper regression (bulk-add, generate-teams, vote), those pytest cases still live in /app/backend/tests/.
